description: A 'tholau' is one hard part of a skeleton, or bone material considered collectively. A body part before it can identify location, as in 'tolae tholau' (leg bone); 'rokili' (spine) is the complete column rather than any one bone within it.
articulatory_notes: Extend the tongue tip slightly between the teeth and let unvoiced air pass for 'th', then draw the tongue back and round the lips for middle-height 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and release laterally into stressed open central 'a'. Keep high-back rounded 'u' in its own final syllable across the hiatus.
usage_notes: Context and a numeral distinguish one bone from bone as material or the skeleton considered together.

semantic_domains.physical: a hard structural part of an animal or human skeleton
